title: Pension Age Disability Payment (Scotland)
jurisdiction: Scotland (in England and Wales this is Attendance Allowance)

effective.uprating: Scottish Ministers' annual up-rating under the Social Security (Scotland) Act 2018 — rates kept at parity with Attendance Allowance

plainly: Scotland's Attendance Allowance: a weekly payment for people over State Pension age who need help or watching-over because of a disability or health condition. Not means-tested — and the corpus's first Scottish entry, so the border now routes both ways.

questions.label: Your date of birth
questions.help: Only used to work out whether you have reached State Pension age — it never leaves this page.
questions.rule: Disability Assistance for Older People (Scotland) Regulations 2024 (S.S.I. 2024/166) reg 17 (age criteria: State Pension age)

questions.label: Do you live in Scotland?
questions.rule: S.S.I. 2024/166 regs 9–16 (residence and presence: this is Social Security Scotland's benefit)

questions.label: Do you already get Attendance Allowance, the daily-living part of PIP or ADP, or the middle or higher care rate of DLA or Scottish Adult DLA?
questions.help: If you get Attendance Allowance in Scotland, you do not claim this — Social Security Scotland transfers you automatically. If you reached State Pension age on ADP, you stay on ADP.
questions.rule: S.S.I. 2024/166 reg 4 (entitlement to other benefits) and Part 15 (transfer from Attendance Allowance)

questions.label: By day, do you need frequent help with personal care (washing, dressing, eating, medication), or continual supervision to avoid danger?
questions.help: The same daytime condition as Attendance Allowance — the two benefits are deliberate twins.

questions.label: At night, do you need prolonged or repeated help, or someone awake to watch over you?
questions.help: The same night-time condition as Attendance Allowance.

questions.label: Have you needed this help for at least 6 months?
questions.rule: S.S.I. 2024/166 reg 5 (the required period condition: 26 weeks)

values.plainly: The rate your answers point to (2026–27: £76.70 where one of the day or night conditions holds, £114.60 where both do — the same figures as Attendance Allowance).
values.rule: S.S.I. 2024/166 reg 26 (amount), as up-rated; parity with Attendance Allowance rates

gates.because: In England and Wales this benefit is Attendance Allowance — worked out in its own answer above. The conditions are deliberate twins; only the door differs.
Pension Age Disability Payment starts at State Pension age. Under it, Scotland's equivalent is Adult Disability Payment.

It cannot be paid on top of Attendance Allowance, PIP, ADP, DLA care or Scottish Adult DLA — you already have the equivalent. Attendance Allowance recipients in Scotland are transferred automatically; there is nothing to claim.
gates.rule: S.S.I. 2024/166 reg 4; Part 15 (transfer from Attendance Allowance)

verdicts.because: Whether your care needs meet the conditions is a Social Security Scotland case manager's judgment on your application — not this page's. On your answers, the rate would be the weekly amount shown. It is not means-tested, it is not counted as income by Pension Credit, and getting it can add the severe-disability amount there.

Not yet: the need normally must have lasted 26 weeks before the claim. (A terminal illness is fast-tracked with no waiting period, automatically at the higher rate — reg 18, with a BASRiS form from a clinician.)

Pension Age Disability Payment is for people who need personal care or watching-over because of a disability or health condition. If your needs grow, come back — the 26-week clock starts when the need starts, not when you apply.

not_asked: your income or savings — it is not means-tested
not_encoded: the terminal-illness special rules (reg 18: no required period, automatically the higher rate, a clinician's BASRiS form — apply immediately)
residence and presence detail (regs 9–16: ordinary residence, past-presence and habitual-residence tests)
the transfer machinery from Attendance Allowance (Part 15 — automatic; awards move without a claim)
short-term assistance while a reduction is challenged (Part 13 — a Scottish invention worth knowing about)

passports.name: Pension Credit severe-disability amount
passports.note: Pension Age Disability Payment is a qualifying benefit — the Pension Credit question above already lists it.

passports.name: Carer Support Payment for the person who looks after you
passports.note: 35+ hours of care for someone on this payment can entitle them — Scotland's Carer's Allowance.

sources_checked.what: Rates £76.70 / £114.60 (2026–27) — identical to Attendance Allowance; terminal illness automatically at the higher rate; not means-tested; AA recipients transferred rather than claiming

Regulation map: reg 4 (other benefits), reg 5 (day/night conditions and the 26-week required period), reg 17 (age), reg 18 (terminal illness), regs 9–16 (residence), reg 26 (amount), Part 15 (AA transfer)
ADP recipients stay on ADP past State Pension age rather than transferring
